item.title: [NBJ] Salesforce Killed The Browser. Every Agent Runs Your CRM Now.
item.itunes:title: Salesforce Killed The Browser. Every Agent Runs Your CRM Now.
item.description:
Full Story w/ Prompt Kit: https://natesnewsletter.substack.com/p/the-5-question-filter-i-run-every?r=1z4sm5&utm_campaign=post&utm_medium=web&showWelcomeOnShare=true

___________________

What's really happening inside the AI agent market when another launch drops every week and the question is no longer what shipped but which of these actually deserves an afternoon of your team's attention?


The common reaction is exhaustion вЂ” but the reality is that the agent conversation has quietly moved from model quality to infrastructure, and most launches fail a simple five-question filter.


In this video, I share the inside scoop on how to cut through the noise:


вЂў Why the best agent news is infrastructure news and the worst is a new destination to migrate to

вЂў How Workspace Agents, Headless 360, Copilot Wave 3, Kimi 2.6, and Perplexity Personal Computer score on the filter

вЂў What Claude showing up inside Microsoft, Salesforce, and Perplexity tells you about Anthropic's real strategy

вЂў Why the switching question is framed wrong and this is actually a layering question


Leaders chasing whichever agent had the loudest launch will fall behind teams that learned to route work across layers based on the shape of the task.


Subscribe for daily AI strategy and news.

For deeper playbooks and analysis: https://natesnewsletter.substack.com/

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: [AIDB] AI Lab Power Rankings
item.description:
NLW introduces the first AI Lab Power Rankings, comparing OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta, xAI, and Apple across compute, enterprise, platforms, models, momentum, and X-factor. The episode explores who looks strongest on paper, who has the most real-world momentum, and why the agent era likely has room for multiple winners. In the headlines: Microsoft and OpenAI amend their partnership, OpenAI comes to AWS, Amazon launches Quick, Claude adds new connectors, and Wall Street reacts to outdated OpenAI growth data.

Make your own rankings: https://ai-power-rankings-rho.vercel.app/https://ai-power-rankings-rho.vercel.app/

April AI Usage Pulse Survey: вЃ https://tally.so/r/LZEyGyвЃ

item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Whittemore
item.itunes:summary:
NLW introduces the first AI Lab Power Rankings, comparing OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta, xAI, and Apple across compute, enterprise, platforms, models, momentum, and X-factor. The episode explores who looks strongest on paper, who has the most real-world momentum, and why the agent era likely has room for multiple winners. In the headlines: Microsoft and OpenAI amend their partnership, OpenAI comes to AWS, Amazon launches Quick, Claude adds new connectors, and Wall Street reacts to outdated OpenAI growth data.

Make your own rankings: https://ai-power-rankings-rho.vercel.app/https://ai-power-rankings-rho.vercel.app/

item.title: [AIDB] The AI Subsidy Era is Over
item.description:
The AI discount is ending as agentic usage drives token consumption through the roof, forcing companies from GitHub to Anthropic to rethink pricing, limits, and compute access. NLW breaks down why usage-based billing is becoming inevitable, what it means for markets and job displacement, and how enterprises can adapt with cheaper models, cost audits, model bake-offs, escape-hatch architectures, and clearer AI cost scoreboards.

5 Moves for Enterprises to Reduce the Cost of Agents: https://play.aidailybrief.ai/episodes/the-ai-subsidy-era-ends/

April AI Usage Pulse Survey: https://tally.so/r/LZEyGy

item.itunes:summary:
The AI discount is ending as agentic usage drives token consumption through the roof, forcing companies from GitHub to Anthropic to rethink pricing, limits, and compute access. NLW breaks down why usage-based billing is becoming inevitable, what it means for markets and job displacement, and how enterprises can adapt with cheaper models, cost audits, model bake-offs, escape-hatch architectures, and clearer AI cost scoreboards.

5 Moves for Enterprises to Reduce the Cost of Agents: https://play.aidailybrief.ai/episodes/the-ai-subsidy-era-ends/

April AI Usage Pulse Survey: https://tally.so/r/LZEyGy

item.title: [NBJ] GPT-5.5 vs Claude vs Gemini: The Real Difference Nobody's Talking About
item.itunes:title: GPT-5.5 vs Claude vs Gemini: The Real Difference Nobody's Talking About
item.description:
What's really happening inside the GPT-5.5 release when everyone is comparing benchmark deltas but missing that the floor moved?


The common story is that 5.5 is a little better than 5.4 вЂ” but the reality is that this model changes what you can reasonably ask a model to do, and I put it through three tests designed to make any frontier model fail.


In this video, I share the inside scoop on why 5.5 is the strongest model in the world today:


вЂў Why the old question was "can the model answer this" and the new question is "can the model carry this"

вЂў How Dingo, Splash Brothers, and Artemis II expose where models actually break

вЂў What 5.5 caught that no previous model caught and where it still needs validation

вЂў Why Codex matters more than ChatGPT for serious work now


Leaders evaluating models on easy tasks will conclude the differences are small вЂ” and they'll be right, but only about the wrong category of work.


Subscribe for daily AI strategy and news.

For deeper playbooks and analysis: https://natesnewsletter.substack.com/

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: [AIDB] How DeepSeek V4 Connects to the US Power Grid
item.description:
Today's episode connects two stories that look unrelated on the surface вЂ” the White House invoking the Defense Production Act around US grid infrastructure, and DeepSeek's long-awaited V4 release. Together they point to a single conclusion, that energy has become the real frontline of the US-China AI competition. In the headlines: Google commits up to $40 billion to Anthropic, the AI trade roars back as hyperscalers push markets to new highs, and Nvidia becomes the first $5 trillion company.

item.itunes:summary:
Today's episode connects two stories that look unrelated on the surface вЂ” the White House invoking the Defense Production Act around US grid infrastructure, and DeepSeek's long-awaited V4 release. Together they point to a single conclusion, that energy has become the real frontline of the US-China AI competition. In the headlines: Google commits up to $40 billion to Anthropic, the AI trade roars back as hyperscalers push markets to new highs, and Nvidia becomes the first $5 trillion company.

item.title: [NBJ] OpenAI Just Gave Every Team a Free Employee. Here's the Catch.
item.itunes:title: OpenAI Just Gave Every Team a Free Employee. Here's the Catch.
item.description:
What's really happening inside ChatGPT's new Workspace Agents launch? The common story is that this is just a chatbot upgrade вЂ” but the reality is more interesting.


In this video, I share the inside scoop on what Workspace Agents actually replaces and where it fits:


вЂў Why this threatens lightweight automation layers, not Claude

вЂў How a plain-English build experience changes who can ship agents

вЂў What workflow patterns consistently work versus consistently backfire

вЂў Where governance becomes the real enterprise unlock


Teams that point AI agents at novel, judgment-heavy work will blame the product when it fails. The real advantage goes to operators who match this tool to repeatable, tool-crossing workflows with a clear output and a human reviewer.


Subscribe for daily AI strategy and news.

For deeper playbooks and analysis: https://natesnewsletter.substack.com/

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.
